title: Gerunds & Infinitives
subtitle: -ing vs. to-V: the verb decides
intro: Many verbs insist on a specific complement form — gerund (-ing) or infinitive (to + V) — and others switch meaning depending on which you choose. 'I stopped smoking' and 'I stopped to smoke' are almost opposites. Getting this right signals exactly the kind of lexical-grammatical control that Band 7+ writing requires.

branches.label: Both (meaning shifts)
children: 'stop smoking' (quit) ≠ 'stop to smoke' (pause in order to smoke)

rules.heading: Gerund-only verbs
rules.body: avoid, consider, deny, enjoy, finish, involve, keep, mind, postpone, practise, recommend, risk, suggest. 'The committee recommended postponing the decision.'

rules.heading: Infinitive-only verbs
rules.body: agree, decide, fail, hope, intend, manage, offer, plan, promise, refuse, seem, tend, threaten, wish. 'Governments tend to underestimate long-term costs.'

rules.heading: Both forms, different meaning
rules.body: stop (stop doing = quit; stop to do = pause in order to); remember (remember doing = recall memory; remember to do = don’t forget); try (try doing = experiment; try to do = attempt); forget, regret similar pattern.

rules.heading: Gerund after prepositions
rules.body: Any verb following a preposition takes -ing: 'interested in learning', 'instead of complaining', 'look forward to hearing', 'accustomed to working'.

examples.sentence: Many governments have promised to reduce plastic waste but have failed to enforce meaningful regulation.
Educators should consider integrating financial literacy into the school curriculum.
Instead of banning single-use plastics outright, some cities have succeeded in reducing their use through taxation.
I remember being told as a child that hard work always pays off.
Companies that stop investing in research risk falling behind their competitors.

band6.text: I suggest to reduce pollution by stop driving cars.
band6.issue: 'suggest' takes -ing, and 'by' takes -ing — two pattern errors in one sentence.

band8.text: I suggest reducing pollution by discouraging private car use in city centres.
band8.why: Correct gerund after 'suggest' and 'by'; also upgrades vague 'stop driving' to precise 'discouraging private car use'.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers use complex gerund phrases as nominal subjects: 'Investing in early-childhood education yields the highest social return of any public spending.' They also wield causative 'have/get someone to do' and 'make someone do' constructions with precision: 'Policy-makers cannot make citizens change behaviour overnight, but they can get them to consider alternatives.'

common_errors.fix: 'Look forward to' → preposition + gerund → 'to meeting you'.
'Suggest' takes gerund or that-clause → 'He suggested going' or 'He suggested that I go.'
'Stop smoking' = quit; 'Stop to smoke' = pause in order to smoke.

practice.title: Complete with -ing or to + V
practice.instruction: Put the verb in brackets into the correct form.

items.prompt: The board decided (postpone) __________ the launch until the third quarter.
Students should avoid (translate) __________ directly from their first language.
She is accustomed to (work) __________ long hours.
We cannot afford (ignore) __________ the warning signs.
I regret (not take) __________ the opportunity when it was offered.
After the scandal, he had no choice but (resign) __________ .
Policymakers should focus on (prevent) __________ the problem rather than (punish) __________ offenders.
items.explanation: 'Focus on' + gerund; parallel form for 'rather than'.

items.prompt: The minister refused (comment) __________ on the allegations.

practice.title: Type the correct form
practice.instruction: Write the verb in brackets in the right form.

items.prompt: I cannot stand (wait) ___ in long queues.
He managed (finish) ___ the report before the deadline.
Before (submit) ___ your essay, proof-read it carefully.
The new law enables citizens (access) ___ their medical records online.
Let me (explain) ___ the procedure one more time.
She denied (be) ___ involved in the incident.
Governments are committed to (reduce) ___ emissions by 2030.
items.explanation: 'Committed to' + gerund (to is a preposition here).

items.prompt: There is no point (argue) ___ with him when he is angry.
items.explanation: 'There is no point in' + gerund (though 'no point arguing' also acceptable).

items.prompt: She suggested ___ the meeting until Monday.
Students often forget ___ supporting examples in their body paragraphs.
He is used to ___ under pressure.
I look forward to ___ from you.
The policy aims ___ carbon emissions by 50% within a decade.
Children should be encouraged ___ their opinions respectfully.
She stopped ___ her phone and looked up.
It is worth ___ the effort now to avoid problems later.
